findings.title: Underlying bridge-out flows use the requested amount instead of the amount actually received

best_agent_match.title: Underlying bridge-out flows assume nominal transfer amount instead of actual received amount

findings.title: Router ignores token and vault return values, so bridge events can be emitted after failed accounting

best_agent_match.title: Router ignores failure return values from mint, burn, and vault operations

findings.title: Router has no on-chain allowlist for bridge assets, so arbitrary token contracts can generate canonical bridge logs

best_agent_match.title: No on-chain token allowlist lets arbitrary contracts emit canonical bridge events

rejected_candidates.title: Inbound bridge executions have no replay protection / unverified transaction hash allows fake cross-chain swaps
rejected_candidates.reason: `anySwapIn*` is already fully gated by `onlyMPC`, and the contract intentionally relies on MPC authorization rather than on-chain source-tx verification. The unused `txs` value is informational only, so missing replay tracking does not materially expand power beyond the trusted MPC role.

rejected_candidates.title: anySwapFeeTo can mint arbitrary tokens and drain the entire underlying vault
rejected_candidates.reason: This is not a distinct contract bug from the router's trust model: `onlyMPC` can already mint and redeem arbitrary amounts through `anySwapInUnderlying`/`anySwapInAuto`. `anySwapFeeTo` does not grant meaningfully more power than the already trusted bridge operator.

rejected_candidates.title: changeVault lets MPC instantly redirect custody of bridged assets
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an explicit `onlyMPC` administrative function for vault configuration. A malicious MPC changing vaults is within the router's trust assumption rather than an unintended privilege escalation introduced by the code.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing initialization of _oldMPC in constructor
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. The constructor sets `_newMPCEffectiveTime = block.timestamp`, so `mpc()` returns `_newMPC` immediately after deployment because `block.timestamp >= _newMPCEffectiveTime` is true.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked array lengths in batch anySwapOut
rejected_candidates.reason: Mismatched array lengths only cause an out-of-bounds revert in Solidity 0.8+, reverting the whole transaction. This is input validation/UX debt, not a reportable protocol vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: No validation of _mpc address in constructor
rejected_candidates.reason: This is deployment misconfiguration risk rather than a runtime vulnerability. If the deployer sets an invalid MPC address, the contract is simply misconfigured at launch.

rejected_candidates.title: Slippage protection can be bypassed via reserve manipulation
rejected_candidates.reason: This is ordinary AMM execution risk, not a router-specific flaw. The contract already enforces `amountOutMin` and `deadline`, and reserve movement before execution is inherent to public on-chain swaps.

rejected_candidates.title: No deadline for anySwapIn functions
rejected_candidates.reason: The plain inbound bridge functions (`anySwapIn`, `anySwapInUnderlying`, `anySwapInAuto`) do not perform price discovery, so omitting a deadline is not a distinct smart-contract vulnerability. The price-sensitive swap-in paths already enforce `deadline`.

rejected_candidates.title: No pausable mechanism for emergency stop
rejected_candidates.reason: Missing pause functionality is a governance/design choice, not a concrete vulnerability in this code.

rejected_candidates.title: Insufficient input validation on path array
rejected_candidates.reason: The library already reverts on invalid paths via `require(path.length >= 2)`. This is only error-message/UX quality, not a security issue.

rejected_candidates.title: No event emitted for anySwapFeeTo
rejected_candidates.reason: Lack of an event affects observability only and does not create protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy risk in anySwapInUnderlying and anySwapInAuto
rejected_candidates.reason: No concrete reentrant path was shown that bypasses `onlyMPC`, duplicates accounting, or steals funds. The candidate is speculative and unsupported by the reachable call graph in this router.
